_comment: Copy to .goal.json to activate. Every gate is OPTIONAL and degradable: if its tool is absent, a required gate fails closed (blocks) and an optional one (required:false) is skipped. controlPlane gates NEVER run autonomously. This ladder turns the round-2 research into concrete, runnable slots — see docs/RESEARCH.md.

gates.name: diff-coverage
gates._why: fail unless changed lines are covered (kills 'added code, no tests')

gates.name: mutation
gates._why: keystone: tests that survive injected faults are theater

gates.name: property
gates._why: property-based / fuzz; shrunk counterexample feeds the next iteration

gates.name: sast
gates._why: semantic SAST; CLI not MCP for a deterministic exit code

gates.name: deps-malware
gates._why: pre-install malicious-package screen (typosquat/install-hooks) — the blind spot

gates.name: license-policy
gates._why: block GPL/AGPL/SSPL creep (AGPL triggers on network use)

gates.name: dead-code
gates._why: self-refilling tech-debt backlog the loop burns down

gates.name: a11y
gates._why: axe-core via Playwright; block on critical/serious (baseline-ratchet)

gates.name: visual-regression
gates._why: unintended UI change (distinct from anti-slop aesthetics)

gates.name: perf-regression
gates._why: benchmark delta vs baseline; noise floor matters

gates.name: loop-eval
gates._why: regression-test the prompts/skills themselves on a golden set

gates.name: deploy-canary
gates._why: CONTROL-PLANE: never autonomous; deterministic SLO-gated rollback owns prod
